Plan: `docs/plan-truth-audit-execution.md` v1.0-rc; review record `docs/reviews/codex-2026-07-02-pivot-crosscheck.md`. **NOTHING IS DECIDED until the owner GO (O1–O8 in plan §9); no build (incl. S0 commit) before it.** | Gates discharged (council + Codex); Accepted-pending-owner-GO | The two mandatory validation gates on the consequential pivot ran and reconciled primary-model-final; the direction's remaining gate is the owner's | `docs/plan-truth-audit-execution.md`; `shared_reasoning.md` (2026-07-02 evening + synthesis); `docs/reviews/codex-2026-07-02-pivot-crosscheck{,-raw}.md`; `decisions_log.md` |
docs/decision-log.md:96:| 2026-07-03 | **FABLE = FINAL JUDGE AT EVERY STAGE + STANDING FULL-LIBERTY LICENSE (owner-directed).** Owner (verbatim core): "ensure all the capabilities are utilized fully post check … take full liberty and full permission … reconcile and do it. also fable be final judge in all stages final call. Fable its own judgement in … giving the standout project." Rulings: (1) every gate verdict, reconciliation, and stage-exit call is made on the Fable seat (fable-override delegate, or a Fable session at the boundary) and is the FINAL CALL — primary-model-final binds to Fable specifically, even while Opus executes; Codex remains adversarial INPUT, never skipped, never blindly obeyed. (2) Fable holds a standing license to detect + apply blindspot fixes, refinements, and improvements on its own judgment toward a standout project — reversible/internal changes proceed without asking. (3) UNCHANGED hard stops: Gemini ≤$5 spend arming, deploy, public posting, git push, name adoption — owner word still required (RULES §12 wins over the license). | Accepted (owner, 2026-07-03) | Concentrates judgment authority on the strongest seat while execution is delegated; matches the existing primary-model-final doctrine with the seat named explicitly | HANDOFF top block (ROUTE + JUDGE line); owner message 2026-07-03 |
docs/decision-log.md:100:| 2026-07-03 | **POST-CHECK ELEVATION MANDATE (owner-directed; extends the full-liberty + Fable-equivalence rows above).** Owner (verbatim core): "ensure all the capabilities are utilized fully post check. Use fable own judgement if any blindspots, fixes, refinements, improvements anything is take full liberty and full permission. Use your own judgement take it to next level in terms quality of work." Ruling: the per-slice loop is **check → judge → ELEVATE** — after the Fable-equivalence review returns PASS, the Fable seat runs an additional improvement pass on its own judgment: capability-utilization audit (is every relevant existing capability — eval harness patterns, red-green discipline, two-register docs, taxonomy coverage measurement, determinism/seeding — actually exercised, not just present?), blindspot hunt, and reversible refinements applied directly without asking. Quality target = standout, next-level work, not merely spec-satisfying. Hard stops unchanged (Gemini spend arming · deploy · public posting · push · name adoption). | Accepted (owner, 2026-07-03) | Converts the standing license from permission into an obligation: elevation is a mandatory step of every slice's close-out, executed on the judgment seat | Owner message 2026-07-03; the two 2026-07-03 routing/judge rows above |
docs/decision-log.md:102:| 2026-07-03 | **DEPLOY DEFERRED UNTIL DESIGN IS FIXED (owner-directed).** Owner (verbatim): "defer deploy aspect, we will do it later once design is fixed." Deploy was already an owner-gated hard stop; this adds explicit sequencing — no deploy work (Vercel or otherwise), no deploy planning pressure in slice ordering, until the product/UI design is fixed and the owner re-opens it. The build program (W0/W1/P1 → M1) proceeds unaffected; deploy remains a documented later phase (prototype-not-service identity unchanged — any eventual deploy is a transient demonstration). | Accepted (owner, 2026-07-03) | Design-first sequencing; removes any implicit deploy deadline from the wedge slices | Owner message 2026-07-03; plan §5 (Pub/deploy stays owner-gated) |
docs/decision-log.md:104:| 2026-07-03 | **W1 EXECUTED INLINE ON THE FABLE SEAT — a recorded, bounded deviation from the delegation ruling (NO-WAIT applied).** Sequence: the W0+P1 Opus builders died on the shared subagent seat limit (raw verbatim: "You've hit your session limit · resets 7:40am (America/New_York)"), the owner confirmed reset, both relaunched and COMPLETED (W0 `1b04766`, P1 `da1e2e7`, each Fable-judged PASS + elevated). The W1 builder then died on a fresh limit (raw verbatim: "You've hit your session limit · resets 2:30pm (America/New_York)") at 10:18 ET — a ~4h wait. Under the NO-WAIT doctrine (wait-states → inline alternatives) + the standing full-liberty license (reversible internal work) + the owner's "resume", Fable executed W1 inline (`5a81440`), keeping the dead builder's sound partial work (C2 guard + category field). **Maker=judge mitigation, named:** (a) the M1 Codex cross-model batch reviews the whole wedge module (never skipped); (b) an independent acceptance-gate pass on W1 is a NAMED OBLIGATION once the subagent seat resets (extra rigor for this deviation, beyond the S-4 per-slice bar, which W1 met: verify green 409+5 + red-green ×4). Delegation resumes for subsequent slices when the seat allows. | Accepted (executed under standing owner rulings; surfaced in-session) | Two owner rulings collided (delegation vs NO-WAIT); the enumerated hard stops were untouched and the deviation is bounded to one slice with named compensating controls | `docs/reviews/w1-verify-evidence.log`; W1 commit `5a81440`; session transcript 2026-07-03 |
docs/decision-log.md:112:| 2026-07-04 | **F1b TAIL EXECUTED INLINE ON THE FABLE SEAT — recorded, bounded deviation (NO-WAIT, W1 precedent).** The delegated F1b builder died twice: (1) subagent seat limit (raw verbatim: "You've hit your session limit · resets 6pm (America/New_York)") → the owner's `resume` confirmed the retry; the SAME builder resumed with context intact and completed ALL build artifacts (58 tool uses); (2) then died on a raw "API Error: Overloaded" BEFORE the evidence log / slice record / same-breath docs. Fable executed that TAIL inline: RED-GREEN ×3 run independently by the reviewer (baseline pin — first mutation honestly recorded too-weak, strengthened; frozen-golden byte tamper; composition split-flip), evidence log, slice record, GLOSSARY +3, PLAIN-ENGLISH row. **Maker≠judge mitigation, named + advisor-ruled (frontier-advisor pre-wrap): the M2 cross-model ceremony runs NOW over the offline F1 module (`896ab59`+`bda6314`) BEFORE any live leg; M2's scope explicitly enumerates (a) the verifier-core ClaimSource "classifier" shared-core touch and (b) the reviewer-executed red-green runs as items Codex must independently re-verify; the owner-gated live classifier run is BLOCKED on M2 SHIP.** | Accepted (executed under standing owner rulings; surfaced at wrap) | Build artifacts stayed builder-authored + Fable-reviewed; the inline exposure is bounded to the documentation tail and compensated by the scheduled cross-model gate | `docs/reviews/f1b-slice-record.md`; commits `896ab59`/`bda6314`; frontier-advisor consult (session transcript 2026-07-04) |
docs/decision-log.md:113:| 2026-07-04 | **M2 DISCHARGED — THE F1 FEE-AUDIT MODULE IS ACCEPTED (eleventh session). Two material spec changes landed under the ceremony:** (1) **`FeeVerdict` gained a fourth state `asserted-passthrough-unverified`** (M2 Codex P1 #1): an over-3% transaction fee carrying the platform-asserted `passthroughDocumented` flag is no longer silently cleared — it surfaces as a non-gating warn (ruleId c-2, U1-provisional, never flips `ok`); shape chosen per frontier-advisor ruling (advisory-warn over a schema "proof" field, which would be fake verifiability). (2) **The pre-registered live-classifier accuracy floor was AMENDED PRE-RUN from ≥0.90 to ≥20/21 — strictly beating the pinned 19/21 baseline; tie = DEFER** (M2 Codex P1 #2): the original floor admitted a baseline tie, contradicting AM-7; amendment dated, strengthening-only, before any live run. Also fixed red-green: parser rejects mixed-month statements (P2); per-line claim ids gained statement-position `L<i>` tags with a conscious golden regeneration whose byte-deltas were verified claim-id/tally-only (P2); the drift-lock now also locks `kind`+`source_clause` (P3). Ceremony chain: batched Codex BLOCK (2P1+2P2+1P3, scope items a/b CONFIRMED) → all 5 reconciled primary-model-final + red-green → mapped confirming pass ALL FIVE DISCHARGED + 1 residual P3 (stale E-1 comment) fixed → independent acceptance-gate **SHIP, all five gates PASS** (its verify handoff executed live: verify exit 0 = 720+5, legacy 306+5; e1 count discrepancy resolved benign — honesty note in the batch record). **The owner-gated live classifier run is now UNBLOCKED for surfacing (not starting).** | Accepted | The cross-model gate restored maker≠judge after the F1b inline tail exactly as the tenth-session ruling required; both P1s were genuine (one statutory-fidelity, one anti-theater) | `docs/reviews/codex-2026-07-04-m2-f1-batch.md` (+2 raw), `m2-reconcile-evidence.log`, `gate-2026-07-04-m2-f1-module.md` |
docs/decision-log.md:115:| 2026-07-06 | **PUB SLICE ARMED + EXECUTED (owner: "except design complete all other tasks. resume"). Four gate-deferred decisions taken as RECOMMENDED DEFAULTS (owner AFK at the ask; each reversible or owner-flippable, none irreversible without the owner):** (1) **Naming = descriptive interim, "Commerce Truth Audit"** — NOT a brand adoption, so the S-11 owner gate is untriggered; live screen found "Assay" collided (assay.tools = live 2026 AI-agent devtools product; bare npm `assay` squatted-abandoned 2013) — S-11 stays open for a future brand pick. (2) **License = Apache-2.0 repo-wide** (code + synthetic corpus; canonical text fetched from apache.org; NOTICE added; matches the UCP/ACP Apache-2.0 ecosystem; closes O6). (3) **Publish act = PRIVATE GitHub repo + push, owner flips public** — the irreversible public act stays in the owner's hands; the public-posting hard stop is honored. (4) **Internal process docs published as-is** (RULES §8 satisfied: README fronts the product). News-moment research (live, 2026-07-06): best anchor = the NYC DCWP recordkeeping rulemaking hearing (July 2026, machine-auditable fee-cap records) + Juniper's trust-as-#1-barrier finding; recorded as timing INPUT for the owner, never a blocker (advisor constraint). | Accepted (defaults-taken, owner-ratifiable; surfaced at wrap) | The owner's word armed the slice; the four in-slice decisions followed the surfaced recommendations exactly; anything irreversible (public visibility, brand name) remains owner-held | `docs/PUBLICATION.md`; `docs/reviews/pub-verify-evidence.log`; frontier-advisor consult (PROCEED-WITH-CONSTRAINTS, 2026-07-06) |
docs/decision-log.md:116:| 2026-07-06 | **PUB RESEARCH LEGS EXECUTED INLINE ON THE FABLE SEAT (recorded, bounded deviation — NO-WAIT, W1/F1b precedent).** The delegated research subagents died on seat limits TWICE: first pair raw "You've reached your Fable 5 limit. Run /usage-credits to continue or switch models with /model."; owner's `resume` = the confirmed retry; the Sonnet-routed retry pair died raw "You've hit your session limit · resets 3:30am (America/New_York)" (the harness safety classifier was also briefly unavailable, blocking Bash). Conversion: S-11 name screen + news-moment scan + sanitization audit run INLINE with live web fetches (npm registry, GitHub API, NYC Rules/DCWP, web search), all dated 2026-07-06 in the records. The frontier-advisor consult itself SUCCEEDED pre-conversion (PROCEED-WITH-CONSTRAINTS; its ordering + sanitization-audit + README-replace + Codex-scope rulings all adopted). | Accepted (executed under standing owner rulings; surfaced at wrap) | Maker=judge on public prose is bounded by the Codex claims-vs-repo-truth batch + independent acceptance-gate that close the slice | This session's transcript; `docs/reviews/pub-verify-evidence.log` |
docs/decision-log.md:119:| 2026-07-06 | **SECOND DIRECTIVE, FOURTEENTH SESSION — "right now i am going to publish and we can we update it later. so complete all other steps now except github, design everyother aspect." (verbatim).** Intent: the owner takes the GitHub publish/flip personally; design stays excluded; every OTHER open recorded item completes now. Executed under that word: (1) the three M2 gate-4 advisory nits deliberately left "for a future slice" are CLOSED red-green (`9ef2d87`): `claimIdPart` separator escape on arbitrary claim-id components (byte-identity on all committed corpus values — goldens byte-frozen, verify 749+6 exit 0), shared loud `makeLineTagger` replacing two silent object-identity maps, stale `FeeFinding.verdict` comment reworded; Codex changed-files review (lightweight loop, RULES §13) = CLEAN on all five challenged paths + 1 P3 accepted-fixed. (2) Suggestions-ledger statuses synced to reality (S-2 shipped-as-reshaped · S-4 practiced · S-5 done at S0 · S-9 implemented at W3). (3) **S-11 alternates screened LIVE** (npm + GitHub + product web search, dated 2026-07-06): Parallax KILLED (GradientHQ live AI-infra tool + funded robotics startup) · Trig KILLED (trig.ai, $10.3M AI-agent platform) · Tare RISKY (live commerce-AI email startup) · Kilter diluted-but-open · **Plumbline cleanest**; trademark/domain checks still required pre-adoption; the pick stays the owner's. Classifier retry stays UNARMED (recorded rule; this directive is not the arming word — precedent ×3). Nothing else remained open. | Accepted (owner directive 2026-07-06) | The one remaining internal backlog (gate-deferred nits + stale ledger + unscreened alternates) is now zero; every still-open item is an owner-only act | `docs/reviews/{advisory-nits-verify-evidence.log, codex-2026-07-06-advisory-nits-raw.md}`; commit `9ef2d87`; suggestions-ledger S-11 row |
docs/decision-log.md:120:| 2026-07-06 | **PROGRAM EXTENSION DIRECTED — AGENTIC LAYER + INTEGRATIONS (owner, fourteenth session; verbatim core: "yes it could integrating slack, email other platforms, mcp so whatever is the trend professionally companies are building and expecting. so what exactly is this called. Applied ai application to the problems or use cases i mentioned. use your call and judgement to make it big.").** Intent (played back + confirmed in-session): the project's PURPOSE is a portfolio-grade showcase of professional AI-engineering proficiency — the kind companies hire for in 2026: agentic/multi-agent AI systems with real integrations (Slack, email, MCP, other platforms), applied to the already-chosen marketplace truth-audit use cases. The current deterministic engine + honesty/evals discipline is the FOUNDATION (verified tool layer), not the headline; the new headline = a vertical agentic AI system operating that engine. Sequence set: ① LIVE research (what companies build/expect in applied/agentic AI, mid-2026 — source-cited, never memory) → ② goal re-fix with the owner → ③ plan + gates (Codex cross-check; council if warranted) → ④ owner GO → build. Judgment license granted ("use your call and judgement to make it big"); hard stops UNCHANGED (live spend arming · deploy · public flip · name adoption · push already routine to private origin). RULES §3 offline-first + §8 dev-tools-not-runtime + honesty/simulated labels bind the new layer exactly as before. | Accepted (owner directive 2026-07-06) | The pivot's audit toolkit stays the verified core; the owner's original agentic-showcase intent (June goal; re-voiced today) becomes the fronted product shape, evidence-grounded before planning | This session's transcript; research digest to docs/research/ (next); suggestions-ledger + plan doc updates at the plan gate |
docs/decision-log.md:121:| 2026-07-06 | **EXTENSION GOAL FRAMING FIXED + SESSION WRAP (owner, fourteenth session; verbatim: "It is a personal project to demonstrate is the goal or the idea, accordingly wrap this session and with proper to do so that other account can continue with it.").** The agentic extension's purpose is a PERSONAL DEMONSTRATION project — showcase applied-AI / agentic-systems / AI-automation proficiency (target roles on record: AI Engineer, AI Specialist, Applied AI Engineer, AI Automation Specialist; boundary: NOT model development/training, NOT no-code-only ease; floor tools: Claude Code/Codex, n8n, MCP, Zapier-class — floor not ceiling). "Make it big" = breadth/depth of demonstrated proficiency across the four surfaces (agent crew · MCP server · Slack/email delivery · n8n workflow lane), NOT commercialization; prototype-not-service identity, honesty rules, $5 Gemini cap, and all hard stops carry over unchanged. NEXT STAGE = PLAN (fresh session, any account): declarative plan over the research digest → Codex cross-check → owner GO before any build. | Accepted (owner, 2026-07-06) | Locks the intent the extension is planned against, so the plan stage optimizes for demonstration value per unit of complexity | `docs/research/agentic-extension-research-2026-07.md`; HANDOFF top block (fourteenth-session wrap) |
docs/decision-log.md:122:| 2026-07-07 | **AGENTIC EXTENSION BUILD ARMED (owner, fifteenth session; verbatim: "except design deploy, GO for it.").** Intent capture (Prompt Intake): GO on `docs/plan-agentic-extension.md` v1.0 — the full slice DAG (A0 registry → A1 MCP ∥ A2 crew ∥ A3 delivery → A4 n8n → AM ceremony); DESIGN and DEPLOY stay excluded by the owner's word (consistent with the standing 2026-07-03 design-first-then-deploy ruling). Owner-call dispositions under this GO: **O-A1 approved** (slice set + ordering as planned); **O-A2 = per-run arming stands** — L-1/L-2/L-3 each still need their own explicit word after their offline gates (RULES §3; nothing live fires under this GO); **O-A3 stays OPEN** (how the private repo is shown — owner act, blocks payoff not build); **O-A4 deferred to A4 entry** — docker is NOT installed on this machine (verified live 2026-07-07); the install ask will be surfaced at A4 entry per the poppler/cargo precedent, else A4 ships as the honest "workflow spec, not executed n8n lane"; **O-A5 dissolved for the build** — A3's email builder is provider-agnostic (RFC 5322 MIME payloads); provider naming binds only at the L-2 transient-demo arming; **O-A6 confirmed** (implementer lane; opus escalation for A2). Hard stops unchanged: live spend arming · deploy (design-first) · public flip · name adoption (S-11) · classifier retry (new pre-registration + word). | Accepted (owner GO 2026-07-07) | The GO fires exactly the plan the Codex cross-check reconciled; every live/irreversible act remains individually owner-gated | `docs/plan-agentic-extension.md` v1.0 §8; HANDOFF A0+A1 build prompt; this session's transcript |
docs/PUBLICATION.md:1:# The writeup — a truth layer for agentic commerce
docs/PUBLICATION.md:7:## The one-sentence pitch
docs/PUBLICATION.md:9:AI agents started ordering food this year; nobody independent checks whether the data they act on is true. So we built the empty seat: an open, deterministic verifier that compares what platforms and agent surfaces *say* about a merchant with the merchant's own records, and audits fee statements against codified law.
docs/PUBLICATION.md:15:1. **Agentic ordering is live.** Since 2026-07-01 you can order food through ChatGPT and Claude via Square; DoorDash ordering inside Google's Gemini assistant began piloting in March 2026. An agent doesn't eyeball a menu and shrug at a stale price — it acts on it.
docs/PUBLICATION.md:16:2. **The protocol layer standardizes *shape*, not *truth*.** ACP (OpenAI + Stripe) and UCP (with its Food vertical) are settling fast as the rails of agentic commerce — and a document can satisfy either spec perfectly while being false about the merchant it describes.
docs/PUBLICATION.md:19:Every seat that could verify the copies is conflicted: platforms won't audit each other, sync vendors would grade their own homework, agent vendors say not-our-job. The independent-verifier seat is structurally empty. This prototype demonstrates what sits in it.
docs/PUBLICATION.md:25:**The demo, in one line (verbatim, single-sourced in code):** "a spec-faithful simulated agent follows a spec-valid but false surface; the verifier catches the surface/SOR mismatch." Run `node bin/check.mjs demo` — deterministic, $0, zero-config; every verdict in the transcript is computed by the real verifier.
docs/PUBLICATION.md:31:The one place an LLM appears is an advisory line-item classifier for fuzzy fee-relabeling cases. Before its label could count, it had to clear pre-registered floors on a held-out split; the floors were committed to git *before* the run, with a no-rerun rule (the split is exposed once scored).
docs/PUBLICATION.md:35:We published the miss. The bar did not move. In a field where "our AI is calibrated" is usually an adjective, this repo treats it as a conjunctive, pre-registered, machine-checked claim, and reports the near-miss as a near-miss. The measurement discipline is the product as much as the verifier is.
docs/PUBLICATION.md:40:- **A prototype run on demand, not an operated service.** The enterprise expansion path is documented, not built.
docs/PUBLICATION.md:59:*Built human-led with AI assistance (Claude Code as planner/builder, OpenAI Codex as adversarial reviewer — development tools, never the product runtime). This work never presents itself as AI-free, and never as AI-built without human direction and review.*
docs/plan-truth-audit-execution.md:4:**Register note (per `docs/documentation-standard.md`):** professional register leads; *plain-English lines are marked ▸*. New terms land in `docs/GLOSSARY.md`. Successor to `docs/plan-multi-agent-execution.md` (superseded for the active program; retained as the provenance spec of the verification spine).
docs/plan-truth-audit-execution.md:11:- **Complication:** Conformance tooling exists (official `ucp-schema`, community validators) — but **nothing checks what a feed/serving copy SAYS against the merchant's system-of-record**, and nothing audits fee statements against the codified caps. Spec-valid data can still lie; agents transact on it; merchants eat the errors.
docs/plan-truth-audit-execution.md:13:- **Answer:** Build **one verifier core with two claim-domain packs**, led by a fast $0-LLM UC-2 wedge (drift engine + one-page evidence-cited report + published torture corpus + spec-faithful demo), with **UC-1 parallelized from week 1** as the AI-depth centerpiece (LLM line-item classification + calibrated judges vs codified rule tables).
docs/plan-truth-audit-execution.md:14:- ▸ *Plain: platforms and AI agents show copies of a restaurant's menu and bills. Copies drift from the truth. We build the referee that catches the drift — first for menus AI agents order from, then for the fees on merchant statements — and we prove every catch with evidence.*
docs/plan-truth-audit-execution.md:18:Demonstrate deep AI proficiency to the delivery-marketplace industry **by benefiting it** (platforms = indirect beneficiaries; company-agnostic; "marketplace integrity infrastructure"). Showcase-first, venture-ready — **"a showcase with venture optionality on two named, observable triggers"** (surface-side truth enforcement; UCP food-schema publication / FTC NPRM). Constraints (all standing): prototype-not-service (episodic runs, no 24/7 ops) · **desktop web only, no mobile** (owner 2026-07-02) · free/free-tier everything except Gemini ≤$5 hard cap · honesty rules (simulated labels; no real platform access/data/impact claims; demo agent = scripted-labeled or Gemini-driven, never Claude/Codex runtime) · legibility = hard artifact constraint (one-command validator · one-page report · demo needs no explanation) · two-register documentation, same-breath maintenance.
docs/plan-truth-audit-execution.md:22:The five-agent council + synthesis (`shared_reasoning.md`, 2026-07-02 evening) confirmed the direction but **reshaped the program**; the seven conditions below are folded into every section that follows:
docs/plan-truth-audit-execution.md:28:5. **Demo slice-C cut** — no fake-checkout build; the demo ends at agent-selects-the-drifted-item; scripted **spec-faithful** agent is the proof (labeled), Gemini is color.
docs/plan-truth-audit-execution.md:29:6. **Category claim demoted** — identity = the mechanism (*"conformance checks the feed against the schema; we check it against reality"*); "truth layer for agentic commerce" = tagline only; venture language capped at the honest sentence above.
docs/plan-truth-audit-execution.md:49:- **As-is:** `lib/agents/{budget,pricing}.ts` (cost ledger + $5 fail-closed), `lib/agents/{gemini,groq}.ts` + `lib/server/env-flags.ts` (provider gates), REPLAY seam ($0 public surface), eval-harness patterns (calibration-lock · differential-oracle · live-gating), Next.js/TS/Tailwind console shell + `npm run verify` toolchain.
docs/plan-truth-audit-execution.md:65:| C7 | Demo needs no explanation | demo claim verbatim (Codex amendment 6): **"a spec-faithful simulated agent follows a spec-valid but false surface; the verifier catches the surface/SOR mismatch"** — never "the agent gets caught" as headline; scripted replay $0, deterministic, labeled "spec-faithful demonstration actor — simulated"; Gemini variant = non-load-bearing color, ≤$0.50, bounded retries, may fail visibly (honest) |
docs/plan-truth-audit-execution.md:66:| C8 | UC-1 classification quality | LLM line-item classifier vs codified NYC rule table: held-out precision/recall published with CIs; calibrated judges (existing bar pattern R-DHON-3); no "calibrated" claim below the pre-registered floor |
docs/plan-truth-audit-execution.md:70:## 5 · Slices + gates (S-4 model — proposed as standing policy, owner call)
docs/plan-truth-audit-execution.md:82:| **D1** | Demo: scripted spec-faithful agent on the drifted corpus (+ Gemini color, ≤$0.50) + conformance-foil beat ("passes `ucp-schema validate`; still lies") | 6–8 d | verify + **owner GO on any live Gemini spend** |
docs/plan-truth-audit-execution.md:100:Synthetic-primary (internally-consistent restaurant SOR + feeds + statements; generator seeded/deterministic) · REAL codified public rules (NYC §20-563.3, SF cap) · live ToS-clean reads only (public specs; own Square **sandbox** via `ITEMS_READ`; recorded UCP catalog fixtures) · zero real merchant data; everything labeled simulated. Operator-voice gap stays open (Reddit blocked ×3) — **owner-assisted pass remains a standing to-do; the plan does not claim operator-demand validation.**
docs/plan-truth-audit-execution.md:107:| O2 | S-4 module-boundary ceremony as standing policy | Adopt |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:9:| **ACP (Agentic Commerce Protocol)** | OpenAI's published specification for merchant product feeds and checkout that AI agents (e.g., ChatGPT) consume to browse and transact; includes feed format and refresh-cadence requirements. | The standard format restaurants/stores use to tell AI assistants "here's my menu and prices" so the assistant can order. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:11:| **Anti-theater floor (AM-7)** | A deterministic baseline whose measured performance an LLM component must BEAT on held-out gold, in an owner-gated live run, before its label ("classifier", "judge", "agent") is earned; ties or losses → the label DEFERS and the step stays a tool. The F1b keyword-rule baseline is this floor for the fee line-item classifier. | The dumb-but-honest benchmark the AI must visibly out-score on unseen examples before we're allowed to call it good — otherwise we don't. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:14:| **Basic service fee** | NYC §20-563 (as amended by LL79/2025): the fee for the service that lets a food service establishment receive delivery/pick-up orders on all the platform's sites and be prominently searchable; capped at 5% of the purchase price per order, or on the calendar-month average (§20-563.3(b)). | The "being listed and findable on the app" fee — max 5%. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:17:| **Category lock** | §20-563.3(d) opening clause: a third-party delivery service may charge a food service establishment only the four permitted fee categories (delivery · basic service · transaction · enhanced service); any other fee is unlawful. | Only four fee types are legal — anything else on the statement is illegal, no matter what it's called. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:19:| **Command-level dry run** | The A4 veracity test (`evals/n8n/n8n-workflow.test.ts`): the committed n8n workflow's OWN command strings are extracted from the JSON and executed verbatim from the repo root, and the final payload artifact must byte-equal a direct registry→builder build. Proves the automation lane's substance without the n8n runtime; running the same workflow UNDER n8n is the owner-gated O-A4/L-3 leg. | The robot's to-do list is run line by line exactly as written, and the result must match doing it by hand — so the list is proven real even before the robot app itself is switched on. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:27:| **Differential oracle** | A second, independent implementation run over the same inputs whose verdicts must AGREE with ours — a check on our own checker. W2's is the official cargo-only `ucp-schema` Rust validator, run in CI when present and SKIPPED-LOUD (agreement UNMEASURED) when absent — never a runtime dependency. | A second referee from a different toolmaker; if the two ever disagree, we have a bug to find. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:28:| **Delivery payload builder** | An A3 pure function (`lib/delivery/slack.ts` / `email.ts`) from a registry tool's canonical report string to a transmittable payload (Slack Block Kit JSON / RFC 5322 MIME) — deterministic, byte-frozen by goldens, SIMULATED-bannered at build time, with NO transport/credentials/addresses beyond `.example` placeholders. Sending = the owner-gated L-2 transient demo under `docs/plan-a3-delivery-safety.md`. | Writes the Slack post or email a deployment WOULD send — but has no send button, no address book, and stamps SIMULATED on every message it writes. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:29:| **Drift** | Any divergence between a serving copy (feed, listing, agent-visible catalog response, fee statement line) and the SOR — price, availability, existence, identity, staleness, encoding, version skew. | The copy no longer matching the original — wrong price, item that's actually sold out, a fee that isn't what was agreed. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:31:| **Drift-lock** | An eval that binds the codified TS rule predicates to the JSON rule-table twin by set-equality both directions: every twin rule id is either implemented (with cap_pct/base/drift_classes matching 1:1) or registered non-statement-checkable with a reason; an unregistered-and-unimplemented id fails, and a TS rule absent from the twin fails. | A test that makes it impossible for the code and the legal rulebook to quietly drift apart — change one without the other and CI stops you. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:32:| **Non-statement-checkable rule** | A codified rule the fee-statement schema cannot verify because it needs data an itemized statement does not carry (e.g. fee-change notice dates for g-1-iv, or a service/searchability obligation) — registered with a written reason rather than faked. | A real legal rule our checker honestly can't test from a bill alone, listed and explained instead of pretended-checked. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:33:| **Enhanced service fee** | NYC §20-563 (LL79/2025): a fee for services beyond the basic service, chargeable only if the platform also offers the basic-service tier; capped at 20% of the purchase price per order, or on the calendar-month average (§20-563.3(d)). | Optional extras (e.g., marketing) — max 20%, and only allowed if a plain basic plan exists too. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:50:| **One-page report (S-9)** | The verifier's output as a single self-contained document — evidence-cited, spec-version pinned, simulated-labeled — rendered two ways over the SAME data: a machine-JSON leg (CLI) and a web view (`/report`). "One page" means one cohesive, printable document per report, not a hard one-physical-page cap; every finding's four receipts stay visible (C2 forbids hiding them), and the print stylesheet keeps a finding from splitting across a page break. | The result written up as one readable (and printable) page — plain sentence per catch, receipts beside it — nothing hidden to make it fit. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:51:| **Orchestration harness passed** | The ONLY label an offline trajectory replay can earn (plan §6): the crew's containment machinery (tool gating at the call site, demo/advisory refusal, human-gate forcing, finding-reference validation) held on every committed case, with model turns replayed from a committed fixture. Explicitly NOT the "agent" label — that requires the owner-gated live run (L-1) clearing the same per-member floors on a held-out split. | "The rails and brakes all work." It says nothing yet about the driver — the AI wasn't really driving. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:62:| **Spec-faithful demonstration actor** | The D1 demo's scripted stand-in agent — labeled "spec-faithful demonstration actor — simulated." It consumes ONLY the published serving copy (its transitive imports are machine-proven to exclude the SOR reference resolver and every SOR fixture — SOR-blindness), applies one fixed scripted intent, and selects the target item at the copy's face value. Deterministic, $0, no LLM; the demo ends at selection (slice-C cut, no checkout). | A rule-following pretend shopping agent that only ever sees the published menu (never the restaurant's till), decides by a fixed rule, and believes whatever the menu says — used to show the drift it can't detect. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:65:| **Tool envelope** | The typed, frozen result every A0 registry tool returns (`ToolResult`, `lib/tools/types.ts`): which tool ran, whether it passed (`ok`, defined as `exitCode === 0` — enforced at construction), its exit code, the honesty flags a consumer must never miss (`demoOnly` on `run_demo`; `advisory`/`earnsLabel` on `classify_and_audit`), and `canonical` — the tool's own named-serializer string output. | The same-shaped little receipt every tool hands back — which tool ran, did it pass, and (when it matters) a flashing "this is a demo" or "this is just a suggestion" flag. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:66:| **Tool registry** | The A0 seam (`lib/tools/registry.ts`): six typed JSON-in/JSON-out tools (`check_feed`, `check_conformance`, `audit_statement`, `classify_and_audit`, `get_rule`, `run_demo`), each wrapping an UNCHANGED engine entry point with a committed input/output JSON Schema. `callTool(name, params)` validates `params` against the tool's schema via ajv BEFORE running it — invalid input fails loud with a typed `ToolInputError`; an unregistered name fails loud with `ToolNotFoundError`. This is the ONE call surface every later surface (MCP server, agent crew, delivery builders, n8n lane) is meant to consume, so they can never bypass the engine's own validation and error discipline. | The one panel of six clearly-labeled buttons every future robot (an AI agent, an automation workflow) presses instead of reaching into the checker's engine room directly — press the wrong button, or press it with a wrong-shaped input, and it refuses loudly instead of guessing. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:67:| **ToS-clean access** | Reading data only through channels the terms of service permit: public documents, one's own accounts/sandboxes, and merchant-permissioned APIs — never scraping walled surfaces. | Only looking where we're allowed to look. |
docs/GLOSSARY.md:71:| **UCP (Universal Commerce Protocol)** | Google-led open agentic-commerce spec (unveiled 2026-01-11 at NRF; Apache-2.0; spec 2026-04-08 live at ucp.dev/GitHub; RFC-2119 normative language). Food vertical co-developed by DoorDash, Square, Toast, Uber Eats — food schemas pending. Catalog capability is a LIVE-QUERY interface (session-scoped), not a static feed. | The Google-side standard AI assistants use to shop and order. Its menu-lookup part asks the store live, rather than reading a saved copy — which matters for what can drift. |
docs/prompts/codex-rescue-template.md:12:- [ ] **Diagnosis only** — include this sentence verbatim in the request: "do not change anything yet, diagnose only."
docs/research/merchant-activation-domain-2026-06-19.md:40:- **First-person practitioner voice is thin** — reddit/X operator threads weren't reachable; blocker categories rest on official docs + a marketplace-onboarding teardown + integration-vendor "what-to-expect" docs (not verified-practitioner). Treat operator-sentiment specifics as practitioner-candidate. Next: `last30days`/`pulse` for Reddit/X.
docs/documentation-standard.md:3:**Status:** standing owner directive (2026-07-02). These are **floor requirements, not a ceiling** — every document meets them; documents may exceed them. `RULES.md` remains the constitution; this standard elaborates §7 (visuals) and the Legibility principle.
docs/documentation-standard.md:28:- One **canonical truth-flow diagram** (merchant system-of-record → copies across surfaces → AI agent → the verifier) is the reused anchor visual across docs.
docs/documentation-standard.md:36:3. **Resonance by narrative, not hype:** the story spine ("the agent gets lied to; the referee catches it before it acts") carries the emotional weight. Adjective inflation is a defect.
docs/documentation-standard.md:43:Before a meaningful doc lands: correct terminology used (not paraphrased) · first-use decodes present · glossary updated with any new terms · diagram present where a flow/decision exists · pyramid-style summary at top · honesty box if public-facing · anti-slop pass if public-facing · PLAIN-ENGLISH.md updated if the change is meaningful.

docs/prompts/claude-task-template.md:32:- (one or two sentences: what to accomplish)
docs/research/ll79-source-memo.md:18:  - basic service fee ≤ **5%** (§20-563.3(b))
docs/research/ll79-source-memo.md:20:  - enhanced service fee ≤ **20%**, chargeable **only if** a basic service fee is also offered (§20-563.3(d))
docs/research/ll79-source-memo.md:33:- **(a) delivery fee — 15%:** *"It shall be unlawful for a third-party food delivery service to charge a food service establishment a delivery fee that totals more than 15% of the purchase price of each online order unless the average of the sum of all delivery fees … in a calendar month totals no more than 15% of the sum of the purchase price of all such orders in such month."* Plus a delivery obligation: *"shall provide delivery to any customer located within at least 1 mile … unless exigent circumstances prevent such delivery."*
docs/research/ll79-source-memo.md:34:- **(b) basic service fee — 5%:** *"a basic service fee that totals more than 5% of the purchase price of each online order unless the average … in a calendar month totals no more than 5% …"* (The bracketed old text — *"any fee or fees, other than a delivery fee and a transaction fee, for the use of their service"* — is shown struck, i.e., **deleted** by LL79.)
docs/research/ll79-source-memo.md:35:- **(c) transaction fee — 3% + pass-through exception:** *"a transaction fee that totals more than 3% of the purchase price of each online order, provided however that a third-party food delivery service may charge … more than 3% … if: (i) such transaction fee is charged … in the same amount as the charge imposed upon the third-party food delivery service for such online order, and (ii) such third-party food delivery service can provide proof of such charge … to both the department and the relevant food service establishment upon request."*
docs/research/ll79-source-memo.md:36:- **(d) category lock + enhanced service fee — 20%:** *"It shall be unlawful … to charge … any fee other than a basic service fee, delivery fee or transaction fee, except that a third-party food delivery service that offers a service for which such … service charges a basic service fee pursuant to this section may charge an enhanced service fee. Such an enhanced service fee may total no more than 20% of the purchase price of each online order, unless the average … in a calendar month totals no more than 20% …"*
docs/research/ll79-source-memo.md:37:- **(e) over-cap refund safe harbor:** *"it shall not be a violation [of] this section where a third-party food delivery service refunds … the portion of any fee … that exceeds any fee cap imposed pursuant to subdivisions a, b or d of this section within 30 calendar days of the final day of the month in which such charge occurs."* (Note: **subdivision c is not listed** — the transaction-fee cap has no refund safe harbor.)
docs/research/ll79-source-memo.md:38:- **(f) search/discoverability for basic-service payers:** *"such third-party food delivery service shall enable a person to search for such food service establishment on all websites and mobile and online platforms … and shall not omit such food service establishment from relevant search results …"*
docs/research/ll79-source-memo.md:39:- **(g) notification + disclosure:** (1) within 30 days of the effective date, notify existing establishments of (i) services offered, (ii) **applicable caps under subdivisions a, b, and d**, (iii) services under the existing agreement, (iv) any fee change (effective **no earlier than 30 days** after notice); (2) establishment may modify services after a fee-change notice or an enhanced-service offer; (3) *"shall disclose, in a clear and conspicuous manner, any fee, commission, or other charge imposed …"*
docs/research/ll79-source-memo.md:40:- **(h) monthly itemized statement:** *"Each month, a third-party food delivery service shall provide to a food service establishment … an itemized list of each transaction … including any fees such … service charged … in the preceding month."*
docs/research/ll79-source-memo.md:41:- **(i)** keyword-advertising consent restriction; **(j)** must allow menus/coupons/marketing inserts with orders; **(k)** anti-price-parity (no forced menu-price matching as a condition of service); **(l)** commissioner fee-cap report due **no later than Sept 30, 2026** (enumerated factors 1–8, incl. violations by section, penalties imposed, restitution recovered).
docs/research/ll79-source-memo.md:44:- **delivery fee** (amended): *"a fee charged … for providing a food service establishment with a service that delivers food from such establishment to customers located within at least 1 mile …"* (the prior carve-out sentence excluding listing/advertising/processing fees is **deleted**).
docs/research/ll79-source-memo.md:45:- **basic service fee** (new): a service that (i) *"allows such food service establishment to receive orders for delivery and pick-up on all websites and mobile and online platforms …"* and (ii) *"enables a person to search for such food service establishment … in a manner that prominently displays such food service establishment by name and includes [it] in results for a search based on customer criteria, such as cuisine type, location, or delivery radius …"*
docs/research/ll79-source-memo.md:46:- **enhanced service fee** (new): *"a fee … for providing a food service establishment with a service other than the service such … service provides … in exchange for paying a basic service fee, a delivery fee, or a transaction fee."*
docs/research/ll79-source-memo.md:47:- **exigent circumstances** (new): *"circumstances outside the control of a third-party food delivery service, such as severe weather conditions or natural disasters."*
docs/research/ll79-source-memo.md:59:2. **Primary — City Clerk certification on the enacted LL79 (S1):** *"passed by the Council on May 1, 2025 and returned unsigned by the Mayor on June 2, 2025."* Under the NYC Charter, a local law neither signed nor vetoed within 30 days of presentation **becomes law by operation of the Charter**; the Council's own record labels this the **"City Charter Rule Adopted"** event.
docs/checklists/prevent-repeat-checklist.md:34:- [ ] Did we **avoid adding a new standing log** unnecessarily?
lib/tools/tools/run-demo.ts:6: * The envelope carries `demoOnly: true` ALWAYS — agents, MCP clients, and the
docs/data-audit.md:51:## Representative Rows
docs/data-audit.md:98:- Contact eligibility or consent fields.
docs/data-audit.md:135:10. There are no fields to prevent duplicate sends or enforce cooldowns.
docs/data-audit.md:140:- Stable unique identifiers present: none.
docs/data-audit.md:196:- `last_email_sent_at`
docs/data-audit.md:198:- `resend_contact_id` or simulated equivalent
docs/data-audit.md:200:Without these fields, Resend integration should not be built because the system cannot prove eligibility, avoid suppressed contacts, or track delivery state.
docs/data-audit.md:254:9. `send_attempts`
docs/data-audit.md:265:The CSV is good enough to demonstrate a toy nudge engine, but not enough to support a reliable agentic operations workflow. The next step should be to create a V1-ready dummy dataset and explicit data model before building Supabase, n8n, Slack, Resend, or Gemini components.
docs/suggestions-ledger.md:3:**What this is:** the standing record of every independent suggestion Claude offers under the owner's judgment license (owner directive 2026-07-02: "till we proceed to build give independent judgement, add your suggestions"). Nothing lives only in chat — every suggestion lands here with its status. The owner disposes; the plan-stage gates (council → Codex → owner GO) ratify anything consequential.
docs/suggestions-ledger.md:9:| S-1 | 2026-07-02 | **Reframe UC-2's lead artifact: an OPEN ACP/UCP conformance + truth-audit toolkit** ("the truth layer for agentic commerce") instead of a merchant-facing prototype SaaS; UC-7 (feed-conformance tooling) promoted from horizon into the lead artifact. | Makes "adoption-grade" literally achievable (the ecosystem can run it), deletes the surface-access/ToS killer, rides the open ACP/UCP timing window (specs weeks old, conformance seat empty), flips spec churn from risk to moat. | ACCEPTED-FOR-PLANNING (decision-log 2026-07-02) |
docs/suggestions-ledger.md:10:| S-2 | 2026-07-02 | **The self-referential demo:** a synthetic restaurant with a real ACP-format feed + deliberately injected drift; a real AI agent attempts the order — transacts wrong *without* the verifier, gets caught *with* it. | The story lands with zero explanation ("watch the agent get lied to; watch the truth layer catch it"); all synthetic, honest, nearly free; doubles as the storytelling arc the owner wanted. | ACCEPTED + SHIPPED AS RESHAPED (D1 2026-07-03: a SCRIPTED spec-faithful demonstration actor, not a live AI agent — the "agent gets caught" framing was banned by plan amendment 6 + machine-checked at C10; the live-agent leg was the Gemini variant the owner DECLINED 2026-07-05; status synced 2026-07-06) |
docs/suggestions-ledger.md:11:| S-3 | 2026-07-02 | **Positioning frame:** "the truth layer for agentic commerce" — UC-2 = the listings domain, UC-1 = the money domain, one verifier underneath; venture story = the OSS-wedge-to-commercial-audit-layer pattern (SSL Labs → Qualys; npm-audit → Snyk). | One sentence carries both modules; answers "is a validator too small?" — the validator is the wedge, the evidence-grade audit layer is the product on top. | ACCEPTED-FOR-PLANNING (with S-1; use in plan positioning) |
docs/suggestions-ledger.md:12:| S-4 | 2026-07-02 | **Process: cut review ceremony to module boundaries.** Per-slice gate = `npm run verify` green + red-green tests; the full Codex + acceptance-gate ceremony only at module boundaries and before anything irreversible. | Slice 1 spent ~1.2M shared-seat tokens across 5 Codex passes largely on wording; the owner already moved this way ("batch the Codex review") — make it standing policy for the new build. | ACCEPTED + PRACTICED (folded into plan v1.0 §5 as the S-4 module-boundary gates, owner GO 2026-07-02; run at M1/M2/Pub; status synced 2026-07-06) |
docs/suggestions-ledger.md:13:| S-5 | 2026-07-02 | **Close out the suspended slice-2 diff:** commit it as-is with its honest degraded-run labels; declare the multi-agent-activation lineage CLOSED as "the spine's provenance" (capability proof); drop the clean-K live re-run — the pivot doesn't need it. | Converts a dangling obligation into the credibility exhibit the new build stands on; frees the repo for the restructure. | ACCEPTED + DONE (executed as S0 at the owner GO — slice-2 provenance close-out committed `a65064b`, 2026-07-02; status synced 2026-07-06) |
docs/suggestions-ledger.md:14:| S-6 | 2026-07-02 | **Edge-case coverage = an enumerated taxonomy with measured eval coverage** (drift types × fee tactics × encoding × availability races × spec-version skew), never an "all edge cases" claim. | "All" is an overclaim our honesty rules forbid; enumerated + measured is stronger — it's provable. | ACCEPTED (standing directive c, decision-log 2026-07-02) |
docs/suggestions-ledger.md:15:| S-7 | 2026-07-02 | **Demo-agent honesty guardrail:** the agent that "gets caught" is a demonstration actor — scripted/simulated (labeled) or Gemini-driven within the $5 cap — never Claude/Codex as product runtime. | Preserves the RULES §8 dev-tools-are-not-the-product line before the demo design starts, not after. | ACCEPTED (standing directive d, decision-log 2026-07-02) |
docs/suggestions-ledger.md:18:| S-10 | 2026-07-02 | **Legibility as concrete artifact constraints:** one-command validator · one-page drift report · a demo that needs no explanation — "complexity spent inside, simplicity delivered outside." | Turns the owner's "not complex outwards" intent into testable design requirements. | ACCEPTED (standing directive b, decision-log 2026-07-02) |
docs/suggestions-ledger.md:20:| S-11 | 2026-07-02 | **Product name recommendation: "Assay"** (the independent test of what's claimed — gold assaying; maker≠judge in one word; CLI-native: `assay check feed.json`; tagline "Assay — the truth layer for agentic commerce"). Alternates in order: Parallax · Tare · Trig · Plumbline · Kilter. Naming principles applied: 1–2 syllables, concrete truth-semantics, CLI-usable, domain-neutral across both modules, no category clichés (no Veri-/Truth-/-ify/AI suffixes). Killed-for-collision but conceptually noted: Tally (split tally-stick; tally.so), Expo (kitchen expeditor; expo.dev), Galley (galley proof; Galley food-data co), Hawk-Eye (keep as pitch analogy only). **All collision notes are UNVERIFIED-from-memory — a live trademark/domain/npm/GitHub check is REQUIRED before adoption (RULES §6); name confirm = owner-gated stop.** LIVE SCREEN RAN at the Pub gate (2026-07-06): **"Assay" COLLIDED** — assay.tools is a live 2026 AI-agent devtools product (same broad space) and bare npm `assay` is squatted (abandoned 2013); alternates not fully screened (seat limits; labeled UNVERIFIED). Interim: the repo publishes under the DESCRIPTIVE name "Commerce Truth Audit" (not a brand adoption — this gate stays open). **FULL ALTERNATES SCREEN RAN LIVE (2026-07-06, fourteenth session — npm registry + GitHub handles + product web search; domains + trademark NOT checked, still required pre-adoption): Parallax = KILLED** (GradientHQ/parallax, live distributed-AI-inference devtool, #1 Product Hunt Oct 2025 + Parallax Worlds, funded AI-robotics startup + parall.ax AI consultancy — same/adjacent space) · **Trig = KILLED** (trig.ai, live London AI-agent platform, $10.3M raised — same space) · **Tare = RISKY** (Tare, live NY AI email-marketing startup for Shopify merchants, funded 2023, LinkedIn `tare-ai` — commerce-AI adjacent) · **Kilter = DILUTED but no same-space product** (Blackbaud-acquired wellness app 2022 + Kilter Systems agtech robots + Kilterly CS SaaS; bare npm/GitHub taken-stale) · **Plumbline = CLEANEST** (no live software product of that exact name found in AI/devtools/commerce; bare npm = stale 2022 Angular test util; github.com/plumbline taken; scoped `@plumbline/…` npm available as with all candidates). Ranked for the pick: **Plumbline > Kilter**; Tare risky; Parallax/Trig/Assay dead. | PENDING (owner brand pick — screen complete 2026-07-06; trademark/domain checks remain before adoption) |
docs/prompts/codex-changed-files-review-template.md:16:- Duplicate-send risk — is the idempotency key real and enforced? Does a re-run double anything?

docs/research/source-lockfile.md:12:| L2 | NYC fee caps as amended: delivery 15% · basic service 5% · transaction 3% (no averaging, no safe harbor, documented pass-through exception) · enhanced 20% (only with basic offered) · category lock (any other fee unlawful) | **LOCKED** (2026-07-03) | Same primary (enacted LL79 §3); verbatim clauses in `ll79-source-memo.md` §2; spot-re-verified against the raw extraction 2026-07-03 (orchestrator) |
docs/research/source-lockfile.md:14:| L4 | DCWP recordkeeping rulemaking is ACTIVE: proposed rule to assess third-party food delivery compliance with the fee caps (LL 103/2021 + LL 79/2025, incl. refund treatment); hearing July 2026 (accommodation requests due 2026-07-09) | **LOCKED** (2026-07-06, relocked for Pub — the publication cites the rulemaking) | rules.cityofnewyork.us/rule/recordkeeping-requirements-for-third-party-food-delivery-services/ · 2026-07-06 · "proposing to establish recordkeeping requirements to assess third-party food delivery service compliance with the fee caps imposed by Local Law 103 of 2021 and Local Law 79 of 2025 and address how refunds impact calculations of compliance" + accommodation-by-2026-07-09 line. Public docs say "hearing July 2026" (the precise 07-16 date is NOT asserted publicly) |
docs/research/source-lockfile.md:20:| L10 | Gemini free-tier figures / pricing (gemini-2.5-flash $0.30/$2.50 per M as of 2026-06-29) | **PENDING-RELOCK** | RULES §6 freshness anchor 2026-06-29; re-anchor at D1 (demo spend arming) per standing practice |
docs/research/source-lockfile.md:24:| L14 | DoorDash ordering inside Google's Gemini assistant began **piloting 2026-03-03** (beta; Pixel 10 / Galaxy S26, US + Korea; app driven in a secure virtual window) | **LOCKED** (2026-07-06) | chainstoreage.com/doordash-pilot-ai-enabled-ordering-google-gemini-app · 2026-07-06; corroborated droid-life.com 2026-02-25 (rollout) + stellagent.ai. Public docs say "began piloting in March 2026" — pilot framing preserved |
docs/research/source-lockfile.md:25:| L15 | ACP = Agentic Commerce Protocol, founding maintainers **OpenAI + Stripe**, **Apache-2.0**, latest spec version **2026-04-17**, status **Beta** | **LOCKED** (2026-07-06) | github.com/agentic-commerce-protocol/agentic-commerce-protocol (repo README + spec/ dir) · 2026-07-06 · "Jointly maintained by OpenAI and Stripe as Founding Maintainers"; license Apache-2.0 |
docs/research/source-lockfile.md:26:| L16 | ~~Juniper Research (April 2026): trust = #1 barrier to agentic-commerce deployment~~ | **DROPPED FROM PUBLIC USE** (2026-07-06) | Only secondary coverage reachable (commercetools/Gladly blogs); the primary report is paywalled → cannot be LOCKED under this file's own definition. The claim was REMOVED from README + PUBLICATION at the Pub gate rather than published on secondary sourcing |
docs/demo-recording.md:12:Commerce Truth Audit — verifier demo (SIMULATED)
docs/demo-recording.md:15:Demo claim: a spec-faithful simulated agent follows a spec-valid but false surface; the verifier catches the surface/SOR mismatch
docs/demo-recording.md:21:BEAT 1 · The agent reads the published serving copy — spec-faithful demonstration actor — simulated
docs/demo-recording.md:23:  ▸ A shopping agent reads the published menu feed — the same data any AI assistant would consume — and never sees the restaurant's own records.
docs/demo-recording.md:29:BEAT 2 · The agent selects an item, trusting the surface
docs/demo-recording.md:31:  ▸ Trusting the feed at face value, the agent picks its target item and is ready to order it at the price the surface shows.
docs/demo-recording.md:35:  the agent is now ready to order at that price — the demo stops here (no checkout).
docs/demo-recording.md:42:  ▸ The verifier checks the exact same feed against the restaurant's system-of-record and flags what the agent had no way to see.
docs/demo-recording.md:46:  verdict: [FLAG] DRIFT ON THE SELECTED ITEM — the agent could not have seen it
docs/demo-recording.md:74:logic. Simulated data, run on demand — not a live service, no platform access.
docs/research/uc1-rule-table.md:17:| Basic service fee | **5%** | purchase price / order | Yes (subd. b) | Yes (subd. e) |
docs/research/uc1-rule-table.md:19:| Enhanced service fee | **20%** | purchase price / order | Yes (subd. d) | Yes (subd. e) |
docs/research/uc1-rule-table.md:24:**Glossary terms** (new; also for `docs/GLOSSARY.md`): *basic service fee*, *enhanced service fee*, *transaction fee*, *purchase price base*, *monthly-average alternative*, *over-cap refund safe harbor*, *category lock*.
docs/research/uc1-rule-table.md:37:| **NYC-563.3-b-1** | §20-563.3(b) | basic service fee ≤ 5% × purchase price, per order | ▸ being listed/discoverable can't exceed 5% | over-cap · misclassification | VERIFIED |
docs/research/uc1-rule-table.md:41:| **NYC-563.3-d-1** | §20-563.3(d) | fee category ∈ {delivery, basic service, transaction, enhanced}; any other fee is unlawful | ▸ only four fee types are legal — anything else is illegal, no matter what it's called | misclassification · relabeling · promotion-deduction mischaracterization · bundling | VERIFIED |
docs/research/uc1-rule-table.md:42:| **NYC-563.3-d-2** | §20-563.3(d) | enhanced service fee ≤ 20% × purchase price, per order | ▸ optional extras can't exceed 20% | over-cap | VERIFIED |
docs/research/uc1-rule-table.md:44:| **NYC-563.3-d-4** | §20-563.3(d) | charging an enhanced fee ⇒ platform also offers (and charges a basic service fee for) the basic service | ▸ they can only bill "extras" if they also offer the plain basic plan | misclassification | VERIFIED |
docs/research/uc1-rule-table.md:49:| **NYC-563.3-f-1** | §20-563.3(f) | paying a basic service fee ⇒ searchable on all platforms, not omitted from relevant results | ▸ if you pay to be listed, they can't bury you in search | — (obligation) | VERIFIED |
docs/research/uc1-rule-table.md:81:  - `id` (e.g. `NYC-563.3-a-1`) · `source_clause` (verbatim clause ref) · `category` · `kind` (`per_order_cap` | `monthly_average_cap` | `passthrough_exception` | `category_whitelist` | `eligibility_gate` | `over_cap_refund_safe_harbor` | `service_obligation` | `disclosure_duty` | `itemization_duty` | `fee_change_notice` | `agency_reporting`)

lib/tools/tools/classify-and-audit.ts:10: * The envelope carries `advisory: true` and `earnsLabel: false` verbatim —

docs/story-arc-content-spec.md:6:- **Repo truth** — the site may claim only what the system genuinely does: the deterministic claims‑gatekeeper (`lib/agents/gatekeeper.ts`), the cross‑family semantic judge (`lib/agents/semantic-judge.ts`), calibration against a labeled gold set, the data model, the recorded live run. Bounded by `RULES.md` (label "simulated", mark **UNVERIFIED**, no affiliation).
docs/story-arc-content-spec.md:45:- L1: the gain, plainly — *AI writes your merchant outreach, and nothing false reaches a merchant, because every claim is checked against that merchant's own data before it sends.* Trust + speed. One line + one sub. No chips, no jargon.
docs/story-arc-content-spec.md:78:- Basis: fix #4 + DoorDash's own "calibrated against human judgment" (translated) + repo.

The seven KEEP paths (`@/lib/agents/{budget,pricing,gemini,groq}`, `@/lib/server/env-flags`, `@/lib/product`, `@/lib/utils`) were left untouched. Verified: no kept-infra file imports a moved file (no `lib → legacy` back-import).

docs/restructure-w0-ledger.md:90:- **Untracked `docs/research/{ll79-source-memo.md,uc1-rule-table.md,uc1-rule-table.draft.json}`** were present in the tree (a parallel P1 slice's artifacts) — **left untouched; not part of W0.**
docs/phase3-prep-slice-plan.md:3:Status: **draft 4 (2026-06-12) — grill complete + Codex Act 2 rounds 1–2 applied.** Round 1 (thread `019ebedb-…`, REVISE, 15 findings — all accepted, folded in) → round 2 (re-review: 8 resolved, 7 partial = doc-consistency + 3 real clarifications #3/#6/#12, all applied as a draft-4 consistency pass). Round 3 (re-review of the round-2 fixes) pending a working seat → converge to APPROVED → owner GO to build. Full critique + arbiter responses in [PLAN-REVIEW-LOG.md](../PLAN-REVIEW-LOG.md). Grill decisions (4 owner-locked, unchanged): Grill-locked decisions: **OQ-1 = rename the v1 CSV to a company-agnostic name** (owner override; `git mv` + the single `scripts/config.py:10` re-pin; content byte-identical; residual content mentions labeled now, publish-vs-exclude decided at Phase 7) · **OQ-2 = commit-fresh** · **PLATFORM_NAME = "Curbside Commons"** (S1 collision check) · **target market = US.** Per the owner's standing order (2026-06-12): **use the Codex account as it is** — no cap-tracking, no date-waiting, no credit/seat advisories; **attempt the run when needed; if it errors, surface the raw error verbatim and stop** (never silently retry or switch; the seat is entirely the owner's domain). Evidence trail: the 2026-06-12 review attempt orphaned with zero output; same-day live smoke tests returned a usage-limit error. The gate is **deferred with this written reason (RULES §9), not skipped** — do **not** build T-003 before it runs, unless the owner explicitly re-decides. Draft 1 (2026-06-09) went to Codex plan review job `bvympilb4`, which returned **NO-SHIP** with the add-alongside finding; this revision applies that and the blueprint-review (`bm0i9bxpy`) findings. Effort: **MAX — auto-routed** (ship-gating + high-risk task; 2026-06-12 auto-adjust doctrine — effort routes by task for both Claude and Codex, superseding blanket MAX).
docs/phase3-prep-slice-plan.md:13:- **T-002's "MERCHANT 20/20" scores only deterministic fields.** All 14 `GOLDEN_MERCHANT_FIELDS` ([scripts/eval.py:25](../scripts/eval.py:25)) are pipeline outputs that do not come from the draft generator: formula `risk_score`, **source-echoed** `risk_level` ([scripts/pipeline.py:294](../scripts/pipeline.py:294)), STEP_MAP blocker/action, and the eligibility/send-gate booleans.
docs/phase3-prep-slice-plan.md:58:  - **structure** — required fields non-empty; subject present; body within length bounds;
docs/phase3-prep-slice-plan.md:70:*Standing-constraint note (owner PROJECT-CONSTRAINTS, 2026-06-12):* the owner's cross-project data-source biases prefer latest/fresh · open · **live** · hybrid — as judgment-weighted preferences, not hard gates. This plan satisfies the achievable mix (open + fresh-at-fetch + hybrid) and documents the trade-off above for why **live** yields here (determinism, golden-label eval, public honesty). The S3 dataset Source-Intake check is performed against **live sources anchored to the build date** (license incl. recent changes · PII · quality · freshness), per the standing vetting rule + RULES §6.
docs/phase3-prep-slice-plan.md:73:- **Synthetic edge-case overlay (quality):** add the safety-path rows by design so the canonical v2 run **exercises every path a viewer should see fire**: ≥1 `opted_out`, ≥1 non-empty `suppression_reason`, ≥1 malformed email (→ expected `review_reason = ineligible_contact`). **The `draft_rejected > 0` path is exercised in the S4 eval corpus, NOT in the live `make_draft` run (Codex round-1 #6):** `run_pipeline()` always calls the clean stub `make_draft()`, which never produces a policy-violating draft, so the guardrail can't reject in the live pipeline today. Planted bad drafts therefore live in the S4 adversarial corpus where the contract/guardrail scores them — the coverage matrix marks `draft_rejected` as an **eval-corpus path, not a demo-run path** (honest separation). **DoD reconciliation (Codex round-2 #6 — the ratified hold/reject/send walkthrough needs the reject path to be SHOWABLE):** the walkthrough demonstrates **reject** via an **S4 eval-corpus replay** — the demo surfaces a planted bad draft and the guardrail's rejection of it ("here is a draft the guardrail caught, with the reason") — labeled as an injected/eval case, distinct from the live deterministic run. This stays honest (no fake live rejection) **and** satisfies the DoD's three-path demo. Phase 3 (real Gemini) is the first point a *live* reject can occur naturally; until then, reject is demonstrated by replay. *(Roadmap/DoD note added so the walkthrough spec matches this.)* Malformed-email handling is **v2-only synthetic**: validation today rejects many malformed addresses upstream, so S3 defines exactly how a planted malformed-email row reaches `ineligible_contact` rather than being silently dropped.
docs/phase3-prep-slice-plan.md:89:- **Blocking secrets hook — a committed, nonzero-exit script, not advisory text (Codex round-1 #10–12).** Implement `scripts/check_secrets.py` (exit ≠ 0 on a hit). Wiring uses the **same mechanism as the git-state check** (below): primary path = run it as a committed close-out step; optional convenience = a tracked `.githooks/` pre-commit + `git config core.hooksPath .githooks` that calls it (since `.git/hooks` is untracked/sample-only). The script is the source of truth. Detector breadth is a **staged-file fixture matrix** covering the forms that actually matter — **`GEMINI_API_KEY=…` and current Gemini/env-key formats** (Phase 3's real key — verify the live format at build time, RULES §6), generic high-entropy tokens, **bearer/JWT-like** tokens, private-key blocks (`-----BEGIN … PRIVATE KEY-----`), `.env` assignments, JSON service-credential blobs, and DB **connection strings** (`postgres://…`). The current `pii_or_secret` detector misses several of these — S5 closes the gap with fixtures proving each blocks.
docs/phase3-prep-slice-plan.md:90:- **Allowlist is NARROW + tested (Codex round-1 #11):** "allow the runtime sentinel ([scripts/eval.py:46](../scripts/eval.py:46)) and security docs" is too broad — a real secret pasted into a security doc would slip through. Allowlist **only exact sentinel fragments / line patterns**, and add a test proving a **real credential in those same files still blocks**.
docs/phase3-prep-slice-plan.md:91:- **Git-state re-derivation as a BLOCKING check — committed nonzero-exit script, precisely scoped (Codex round-1 #12 + round-2 clarifications):** `scripts/check_git_state.py` re-derives `git log -1` and **exits ≠ 0** when a tracked state-doc's *current-state* HEAD line disagrees with live git — mechanical, not advisory (the advisory version is what allowed the drift 4×). Two ambiguities round 2 flagged, now pinned:
docs/phase3-prep-slice-plan.md:92:  - **What it parses (avoid false alarms on history):** it checks only the **explicitly-marked current-state lines** in the named files — the `Git (re-derived …): … HEAD = <sha>` line in `PROJECT_STATE.md` + `CURRENT_TASK.md` + `HANDOFF.md` — via a fixed marker (e.g. a `<!-- git-state: HEAD -->` sentinel or the existing "HEAD = " prefix on those lines). It does **not** scan prose or historical log/SHA mentions (which legitimately name old commits).
docs/phase3-prep-slice-plan.md:99:Any live Gemini/model call · real secrets · network at *runtime* (the one-time S3 dataset fetch is a build-time action, recorded + frozen) · new pip dependencies · modifying `tests/test_t001.py` / `tests/test_t002.py` (additive-only) · editing the v1 source CSV **content** or its golden/baseline (the OQ-1 filename rename via `git mv` + the `config.py:10` re-pin is the sole, owner-directed exception — content stays byte-identical) · real sends · 24/7/hosting/ops machinery (prototype identity) · paid tools (free-first; Gemini <$5 enters only at Phase 3). Phase 3 itself is the **next** task and is FULL workflow.
docs/phase3-prep-slice-plan.md:108:- **Hooks (S5):** **`scripts/check_secrets.py` (committed, exit ≠ 0)** blocks each form in the fixture matrix — incl. `GEMINI_API_KEY=…`, bearer/JWT, private keys, `.env`, JSON creds, connection strings (#10) — allows a clean commit + the **narrowly-allowlisted** sentinel, and **still blocks a real credential planted in an allowlisted file (#11)**; **`scripts/check_git_state.py` (committed, exit ≠ 0)** fails when a state-doc HEAD line ≠ live `git log -1` and passes when they match (#12).
docs/phase3-prep-slice-plan.md:131:- Are the adversarial cases **representative of real LLM failure modes**, or just more hand-written strings?
docs/phase3-prep-slice-plan.md:132:- Does the secrets hook have false-negative gaps; is it location-anchored (no false positives on the repo's own security docs/sentinel)?
docs/phase3-prep-slice-plan.md:133:- Does the git-state check actually **block**, or is it advisory theater?
docs/WHY.md:21:**Why deterministic-first, LLM-second.** The risky, audited decisions (risk score, blocker, eligibility, send/hold) are pure deterministic code; the LLM only drafts prose. *Rejected:* LLM-scores-and-decides. *Cost:* a model deciding who to contact and whether to send is non-reproducible and unauditable — you can't defend or regression-test it. Deterministic-first makes every decision replayable and gated.
docs/WHY.md:23:**Why a bounded LLM, not an autonomous agent.** The model gets schema-constrained, single-shot drafting behind guardrails. *Rejected:* an agentic loop with tools/autonomy. *Cost:* autonomy multiplies the failure surface (runaway loops, tool abuse, unbounded spend) for little value here, and the AI-SDR market shows fully-autonomous outbound collapsing (75–90% 3-month churn). Bounded + evaluated is better value-per-risk; the "no autonomous agent" stance is deliberate maturity.
docs/WHY.md:29:**Why this eval design.** Deterministic graders (structure · state-consistency · policy · no-leakage) score every draft, each paired with a corrupted record it must catch. *Rejected:* an LLM-judge as the primary gate, or eyeballing quality. *Cost:* an uncalibrated judge is a rubber stamp, and "looks good" doesn't regression-test. A grader that can't fail is theater — the corrupted-record tests prove the teeth. (An LLM-judge for *semantic* unsupported claims is a calibrated, key-gated secondary, added with live AI.)
docs/WHY.md:31:**Why the injection cut (placeholder substitution).** The untrusted business name is never sent to the model; it addresses a `{{MERCHANT}}` placeholder, and the real name is substituted only after generation. *Rejected:* sanitize the name and pass it in. *Cost:* sanitizing strips control chars but not instruction-like wording ("…IGNORE ALL PREVIOUS…"), so passing it in keeps the injection surface open. Not crossing untrusted text into the prompt closes it by construction.
docs/WHY.md:43:**Why human-in-the-loop (hold / reject / send).** High-risk drafts are held for a person; only clean, low-risk ones simulate-send. *Rejected:* auto-send everything the gatekeeper passes. *Cost:* the AI-outreach wave's damage (deliverability, trust, liability) comes from autonomous sending; a human gate is the practitioner-validated control and a deliverability defense.
docs/WHY.md:45:**Why these metrics (activation funnel · blocker mix · hold/send rates).** They answer "what would this move" for an ops team, co-equal with the AI view. *Rejected:* AI-quality metrics only. *Cost:* a tool that shows model scores but not business impact reads as an engineering demo, not an adoptable product. (Labeled simulated — no real-impact claim.)
docs/WHY.md:55:**Why we label everything "simulated / synthetic" and never claim real impact.** Credibility with a real evaluator depends on it; one overclaim discredits the whole artifact. *Rejected:* presenting the demo as a live product. *Cost:* it would be false and self-defeating. Human-led, AI-assisted, professionally reviewed.
docs/WHY.md:57:**Honest production gaps (the adoption boundary).** Adoption-*grade* means the architecture, controls, evals, the real-data adapter, and a documented adoption path are credible enough to inherit — **not** "production-ready." Known gaps: auth/multi-tenancy, real integrations (Slack/email/CRM), persistence/observability at scale, a **calibrated LLM-judge** for semantic unsupported-claims (the deterministic eval — incl. the no-leakage grader — caught register/disclosure leaks in 3 of the 5 parsed live drafts, and the gatekeeper now blocks them; a calibrated *semantic* judge remains unbuilt), and the deeper blocker root-causes that need instrumentation (named in `lib/domain/diagnosis.ts`, not faked). A marketplace inherits it by swapping the real layer of the hybrid dataset for its own export against the adapter's documented contract.
docs/audits/build-process-compliance-audit.md:28:**Re-derived git state — snapshot at audit start, 2026-06-04 (before this audit made its own doc edits):** branch `main`; `HEAD = dc7d131` ("Clean up T-002 merge status"); working tree clean *at that point*. This is a point-in-time snapshot, **not** a standing claim: this audit's own corrections plus the 2026-06-04 decision-log ratifications are themselves an **uncommitted batch**, so a reader during that window will see a dirty tree. Always re-derive from live `git status` / `git log` (`RULES.md` §1, the source of truth) rather than trusting this line.
docs/audits/build-process-compliance-audit.md:63:The T-001 arc in particular is textbook: a **NO-SHIP** on the missing send-gate was caught at plan review, fixed, and re-validated before any code shipped.
docs/audits/build-process-compliance-audit.md:94:| 4 | doc test count 33→35; routable Gmail fixture | docs say 35 (verified 35); fixture replaced by sentinel |
docs/audits/build-process-compliance-audit.md:95:| 5 | aggregate gate didn't validate `simulated_send_events` | `_check_aggregate` now **compares** vs `simulated_send_events_count: 12` (eval.py:459-465); **E4** asserts `== 12` |
docs/audits/build-process-compliance-audit.md:96:| 6 | live-key-shaped fixture; guardrail/dict desync; fail-fast diagnostics | No real secrets or routable credentials committed; the secret-pattern regression uses synthetic/non-routable sentinel data (assembled at runtime); data-dictionary §9 synced; fail-fast implemented |
docs/audits/build-process-compliance-audit.md:106:- **Secrets:** none. No real secrets or routable credentials were committed; the secret-pattern regression uses synthetic/non-routable sentinel data. The one secret-shaped test case uses sentinel `__REGRESSION_PII_API_KEY_ASSIGNMENT__`, and the secret-shaped string is assembled from fragments **at runtime** rather than stored as a literal (`RULES.md` §11 upheld).
docs/audits/build-process-compliance-audit.md:125:1. **Git-state / handoff-line drift (dominant, recurring).** Stale ≥3× in T-001 → a rule was created specifically for it (playbook: "*this rule exists because the handoff git-line went stale three times in T-001*") → it drifted again **during** T-002 (Codex round 3) → and **again at the merge gate** (this audit). **The control exists; it was not enforced at task-close.** That reframing matters: the fix is *enforcement at the gate*, not another standing doc.
docs/audits/build-process-compliance-audit.md:135:1. **Re-derive git state at task-close, every time.** Make the prevent-repeat-checklist git-state step a *blocking* close-out action, not advisory — the control already exists; enforce it. (Addresses the #1 recurring failure.)
docs/audits/build-process-compliance-audit.md:138:4. **Resolve the two long-standing hygiene follow-ups** *before* adding Phase-3 surface: `out/` volatile-log tracking policy; and the **enforcement-hooks** decision (CSV-immutability / secrets-blocking). Phase 3 introduces live model calls + secrets, so the secrets-hook decision is now timely, not optional.
docs/tooling-and-skills-usage.md:3:> **Purpose:** a queryable record of *how each tool, skill, and agent was used and how useful it was* on this project — so anyone (especially the owner) can ask "how was [graphify / mem / caveman / Codex / X] utilized?" and get a grounded answer. Complements the chronological `docs/task-log.md` (RULES §5) with a **tool-indexed** view.
docs/tooling-and-skills-usage.md:5:> **Maintenance rule (standing):** whenever a tool/skill/agent is used — *or evaluated and deliberately not adopted* — add/append a row with **what for · how useful · basis**. Keep it lightweight (one row per tool; append notes). Owner-requested 2026-06-09.

Findings fold into the agentic foundation layer. |

docs/tooling-and-skills-usage.md:58:| **advisor** (stronger-model reviewer) | Used ×3 | 2026-06-28 | (1) before the run — reshaped the approach (three different mechanisms per agent, not one ablation; reuse the existing tune/test partition for K; the snapshot-overwrite + arm-check blindspots); (2) after run #1 — proved the determined-fallback, decoupled it from the Router regression-risk, named the pre-reg-vs-measured gap + the Codex-currency process gap; (3) after run #3 — the decisive **two-deliverable** reframe (labels CLEAN + run-independent vs convergence INCOMPLETE/K-vacuous), elevated the parse-failure as a drafter finding, sequenced drafter-fix-before-rerun. | Highest-leverage at every approach boundary; each call caught a real structural/honesty risk before it shipped. |
docs/tooling-and-skills-usage.md:61:| **acceptance-gate** (subagent, ran ×2) | Used | 2026-06-28 | The ship gate (BLOCK on 2 process gates → re-judged with the Codex+verify evidence → SHIP 5/5). | Independent maker≠judge; file-verified the fixes rather than trusting the coordinator; flagged the metric-change-without-its-own-red-green for the next slice. |
docs/tooling-and-skills-usage.md:63:| **the live harness** (`evals/agent-loop.live.test.ts`, 3 runs) | Used | 2026-06-28 | The metered cross-family run (Gemini drafter ⊥ Groq critics). | ~$0.046 total over 3 runs (« $5). Run #2 surfaced an over-strict assert; run #3 (corrected metric) surfaced the ~75% Gemini structured-output parse-failure finding. |
docs/tooling-and-skills-usage.md:70:| **acceptance-gate** (subagent) | Used | 2026-06-29 | The ship gate re-stamp after gate-2 cleared. | Independent maker≠judge; gates 1-5 PASS; its only BLOCK was a stale-state-doc defect (docs still said gate-2 NAMED-OPEN + sent the next session to re-run a discharged Codex pass) — caught a real ~200k-token waste + a self-contradicting record before commit. It file-verified that the gate-2 rewordings landed ("true upper bound" gone from all lib/evals .ts) rather than trusting the coordinator. |
docs/tooling-and-skills-usage.md:75:| **Blindspot probes (inline Bash)** | Used | 2026-07-03 | Pre-handoff checks: poppler (NOT installed — HANDOFF P1 line annotated), .env/.env.example present + ignored, untracked harness scaffolding → .gitignore'd, live `npm run verify` baseline re-run. | Answered the HANDOFF's open "[poppler now?]" question before the build session hits it; keeps the per-slice diff gates clean. |
docs/tooling-and-skills-usage.md:76:| 2026-07-06 | Pub slice: frontier-advisor (pre-approach ruling, adopted ×5) · research-specialist ×4 (all seat-limited; inline conversion) · codex-guarded ×6 (smoke + 5-pass chain to SHIP) · acceptance-gate agent (SHIP) · WebSearch/WebFetch inline (name screen, news moment, 6 lockfile relocks) | publication ceremony end-to-end |
docs/research/source-intake-review.md:27:- **Key ideas:** "dynamic workflow" = a team of agents spun up by the word `WORKFLOW`. Six-part anatomy: corpus → role/goal → explicit extraction spec → **fan-out (parallel)** → **verify pass (fact-check, report survived-vs-cut)** → one self-contained HTML report with **source on every line**. Three use cases (log-based upgrade guide, X-scraper research report, `~/.claude` setup audit) + a build-your-own template. Requires Claude Code **≥ 2.1.154** with dynamic workflows enabled, paid plan; Prompt 2 needs an Apify token.
